name: Standing Decompression
description: A foundational Functional Patterns exercise that uses gravity and intentional posture alignment to decompress the spine while standing. Focuses on creating space between vertebrae through proper stacking and breathing.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing forward
Shift weight slightly forward onto the balls of the feet
Engage the glutes gently to tuck the pelvis into a neutral position
Imagine a string pulling the crown of your head toward the ceiling
Breathe deeply into the ribcage, expanding laterally
Hold this aligned position for 60 seconds, focusing on creating length through the spine

name: Hip Shift Correction
description: Addresses lateral hip imbalances by training proper weight distribution and hip alignment. Essential for correcting asymmetrical gait patterns and reducing compensatory movement.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart in a neutral stance
Place hands on the iliac crests (top of the hip bones) to monitor alignment
Slowly shift your hips to the left while keeping the ribcage stable
Return to center, then shift to the right
Identify which side feels restricted and spend more time on that direction
Perform 10 controlled shifts per side, maintaining level shoulders throughout

name: Gait Arm Swing Drill
description: Trains the reciprocal arm-leg coordination that defines efficient human gait. Emphasizes contralateral patterning to improve walking and running mechanics.
instructions: Stand in a staggered stance with your right foot forward and left foot back
Drive your left arm forward and right arm back, mimicking a walking pattern
Focus on the rotation through the thorax — the torso should rotate slightly with each swing
Keep the core engaged and avoid excessive lateral sway
Perform 15 controlled swings, then switch the lead foot
Progress by adding resistance bands or increasing speed while maintaining form

name: Thoracic Rotation
description: Mobilizes the thoracic spine to restore rotational capacity essential for gait, throwing, and everyday movement. Targets the mid-back region that commonly becomes stiff from sedentary lifestyles.
instructions: Begin in a half-kneeling position with your right knee down
Place your right hand behind your head with the elbow pointing out
Rotate your torso to the right, leading with the elbow toward the ceiling
Return slowly to the starting position with control
Perform 10 rotations, then switch sides
Focus on isolating the rotation to the thoracic spine — avoid moving the hips

name: Single-Leg Stance
description: Develops unilateral balance and hip stability fundamental to all single-leg activities including walking and running. Trains the stabilizers of the ankle, knee, and hip in a functional standing position.
instructions: Stand tall with feet together and arms relaxed at your sides
Shift your weight onto your left foot, grounding through the big toe, little toe, and heel
Slowly lift the right foot off the ground, bringing the knee to hip height
Hold for 30 seconds, keeping the standing hip level — avoid dropping the lifted side
Switch legs and repeat
To progress, close your eyes or perform on an unstable surface

name: Myofascial Ball Release — Lower Back
description: Uses a lacrosse or myofascial release ball to target tight fascia and trigger points in the lower back and QL (quadratus lumborum) region. Prepares the tissue for better movement quality.
instructions: Place a lacrosse ball on the floor and lie on your back with the ball positioned between your spine and the edge of the erector muscles
Start just above the pelvis on one side
Apply comfortable pressure by relaxing your body weight onto the ball
Slowly roll up and down, pausing on any tender spots for 20–30 seconds
Spend about 60 seconds per side
Avoid placing the ball directly on the spine — stay on the muscular tissue beside it

name: Pelvic Tilt Correction
description: Restores neutral pelvic alignment by training awareness and control of anterior and posterior pelvic tilt. Critical for addressing lower back pain and improving foundational posture.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor
Place your hands on the front of your hip bones to feel the tilt
Slowly tilt the pelvis posteriorly, pressing the lower back into the floor — exhale as you flatten
Then tilt anteriorly, creating a small arch in the lower back — inhale as you arch
Find the neutral position between these two extremes
Repeat the cycle 10 times, then hold neutral for 20 seconds

name: Cross-Body Throwing Pattern
description: An advanced Functional Patterns movement that integrates the full kinetic chain through a rotational throwing pattern. Trains how the body naturally generates and transfers force across contralateral slings.
instructions: Stand in a staggered stance with your left foot forward, holding a light medicine ball or mimicking the motion without load
Begin with the ball at your right hip, torso rotated to the right
Initiate the throw from the ground up — drive through the right foot, rotate the hips, then the thorax
Release or simulate release across the body toward the upper left
Decelerate with control, engaging the core to stop the rotation
Perform 8 reps per side, focusing on sequential activation from feet to hands
